![atrium health home](https://cdn.atriumhealth.org/-/media/chs/images/logo/logo-atrium-white-svg.svg?rev=317f000e40a749f0b116caa25939a4fb&h=65&w=290&la=en&hash=FE8BAD51C8E31024C20938B4BF7A68EA)

# Benefits of Minimally Invasive Surgery

By using minimally invasive surgery (MIS) techniques, Carolinas Medical Center offers our patients many benefits over traditional surgeries. They include:

## Less Pain

MIS procedures cause less post-operative pain and discomfort. Studies have shown that patients undergoing MIS procedures report less pain and require smaller doses of pain relievers than patients undergoing traditional surgeries.

## Shorter Hospital Stay

Shorter hospital stay and quicker return to normal activities. Patients who undergo MIS procedures are usually able to go home sooner. And, in many cases, the patient is able to return to normal activities and work more quickly.

## Less Scarring

MIS procedures require smaller incisions--which means smaller, less noticeable scars. The scars that do form as a result of MIS typically have a less jagged edge--giving them a more appealing look.

## Less Injury to Tissue

Most traditional surgeries require a long incision. This incision usually has to be made through muscle. Muscle needs a significant time to heal after surgery. Because there are no long incisions in MIS, surgeons often do not have to cut through muscles to complete the procedure--leading to less tissue damage and quicker recovery.

## Higher Accuracy Rate

A higher accuracy rate for most procedures. Because MIS procedures use video-assisted equipment, the surgeon has better visualization and magnification of internal organs and structure. For patients, this translates into a more accurate and definitive procedure.

## Is MIS Right for You?

At Carolinas Medical Center, minimally invasive procedures are used whenever possible. Although some patients are not candidates for minimally invasive surgery, it is important that you seek a second opinion whenever discussing the surgical options that are available to you.

# How Does Minimally Invasive Surgery Benefit Recovery Times?

![](https://www.primesurgicalsuites.com/hubfs/Prime%20Assets-2019/post_calendar-grey_solid.svg)
![](https://www.primesurgicalsuites.com/hubfs/Prime%20Assets-2019/post_share-square-solid.svg)

In our busy lives, many patients are worried about how quickly they can bounce back after surgery. Minimally invasive surgery (MIS) has revolutionized the medical field by offering procedures that not only treat conditions effectively but also promote quicker recovery times. For those seeking to understand how MIS can improve their recovery from surgery, this blog provides valuable insights into the numerous benefits of this advanced surgical approach.

## What is Minimally Invasive Surgery?

Minimally invasive surgery refers to a variety of surgical techniques that involve smaller incisions compared to traditional open surgery. These procedures utilize specialized instruments and often involve the use of a laparoscope, a thin tube with a camera, allowing surgeons to operate with enhanced precision.

Common types of MIS include laparoscopic surgery, [robotic-assisted surgery](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/treatments/22178-robotic-surgery), and endoscopic surgery. Compared to traditional surgery, MIS offers a range of advantages, particularly in terms of recovery from surgery.

## How Minimally Invasive Surgery Improves Recovery Time

One of the most compelling reasons patients opt for minimally invasive surgery is its positive impact on recovery times. Here's how MIS can significantly improve your recovery from surgery:

### Reduced Tissue Damage and Smaller Incisions

MIS involves smaller incisions, which means less damage to the surrounding tissues. This reduction in trauma facilitates faster healing and reduces the time required to recover fully.

### Lower Risk of Infection

Smaller incisions also mean fewer opportunities for bacteria to enter the body, resulting in a lower risk of postoperative infections. This contributes to a safer recovery period.

### Decreased Pain and Discomfort

Patients who undergo MIS typically experience less pain and discomfort post-surgery. This not only makes the recovery process more comfortable but also reduces the need for pain medication, which can have its own side effects.

### Faster Return to Normal Activities

Due to the minimally invasive nature of the procedures, patients often find that they can resume their normal activities much sooner than those who undergo traditional surgery. This quicker return to daily life is a significant benefit for many individuals.

## Benefits of Minimally Invasive Surgery Beyond Recovery Time

While improved recovery times are a primary advantage of MIS, there are several other benefits that make it a preferred choice for many patients:

### Enhanced Precision and Control for Surgeons

The advanced technology used in MIS provides surgeons with greater precision and control, leading to [better surgical outcomes](https://www.primesurgicalsuites.com/blog/how-safe-is-an-ambulatory-surgical-center).

### Shorter Hospital Stays and Lower Healthcare Costs

MIS often requires shorter hospital stays, which can significantly reduce healthcare costs for patients and their families.

### Better Cosmetic Outcomes

With smaller incisions, patients benefit from less scarring and better cosmetic results post-surgery.

### Lower Risk of Complications

The precision and reduced trauma associated with MIS lead to a lower risk of complications, contributing to a safer overall experience.

Studies have shown that patients who undergo minimally invasive surgery typically experience a 20-30% reduction in recovery times compared to traditional surgery. For instance, a study published in the Journal of the American Medical Association found that patients who had laparoscopic procedures had [shorter hospital stays and faster recovery times](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4686422/#:~:text=In%20the%20case%20of%20cholecystectomy,been%20shown%20in%20most%20indications.).

## How to Prepare for Minimally Invasive Surgery

Preparation is key to ensuring a smooth and successful minimally invasive surgery. Here are some preoperative guidelines to consider:

### Follow Your Surgeon's Instructions

### Healthy Lifestyle Choices

### What to Expect on the Day of Surgery

## Post-Surgery Care and Tips for Faster Recovery

Following your minimally invasive surgery, proper postoperative care is essential for a quick recovery. Here are some tips to help you improve your recovery time:

### Adhere to Postoperative Instructions

### Attend Follow-Up Appointments

### Stay Active, But Be Cautious

## Conclusion

Minimally invasive surgery offers numerous benefits, particularly when it comes to improving recovery times. With reduced pain, lower infection risks, and quicker returns to normal activities, it's no wonder more patients are opting for MIS. If you're considering surgery and want to learn more about how minimally invasive techniques can benefit you, we invite you to [connect with Prime Surgical](https://www.primesurgicalsuites.com/contact-us?hsLang=en) in Granite Falls.

[Our team](https://www.primesurgicalsuites.com/) is here to provide the guidance and solutions you need for a successful and speedy recovery.

Patient care is at the heart of everything Prime Surgical Suites does. Our dynamic group of expert physicians leads the way by providing advanced techniques and facilities, an enlightened approach to affordable treatment and care, and a profoundly personal connection to everyone we care for. [Contact us today](https://www.primesurgicalsuites.com/contact-us?hsLang=en) to schedule an opportunity to learn more.

# Benefits of Minimally Invasive Surgery

![paragon-minimally-invasive-surgery](https://paragonsurg.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/minimally-invasive-surgery-1024x1024.png)

When facing surgery, one of the most common concerns patients have is the level of pain they might experience afterward. Fortunately, advances in surgical techniques have significantly reduced the discomfort associated with many procedures. Minimally invasive surgery (MIS) has emerged as a preferred approach, offering numerous benefits over traditional open surgeries, particularly in terms of reducing postoperative pain. At Paragon Surgical Specialists in Concord, NC, the experienced team of board-certified surgeons is dedicated to providing patients with state-of-the-art, minimally invasive surgical options that lead to faster recovery and less pain.

#### What is Minimally Invasive Surgery?

Minimally invasive surgery involves performing procedures through small incisions rather than the large cuts typical of traditional surgery. Surgeons use specialized instruments, including tiny cameras and precision tools, to operate with great accuracy. This technique is commonly used in various types of surgeries, from general to specialized procedures, and has become increasingly popular due to its many benefits.

Some examples of minimally invasive surgeries offered by Paragon Surgical Specialists include:

#### How Minimally Invasive Surgery Reduces Pain

One of the primary reasons minimally invasive surgery is less painful than traditional surgery is the size and number of incisions. Smaller incisions cause less trauma to the body, which in turn reduces the amount of pain experienced after the procedure. The reduced trauma also means there is less disruption to muscles, nerves, and tissues surrounding the surgical site.

Additionally, minimally invasive techniques allow for more precise surgery, leading to fewer complications and less postoperative inflammation—another significant source of pain.

#### Postoperative Pain Management at Paragon Surgical Specialists

While minimally invasive techniques may reduce the pain associated with surgery, effective pain management is still a critical component of postoperative care. At Paragon Surgical Specialists, the medical team works closely with patients to create personalized pain management plans that address individual needs and ensure a comfortable recovery.

These pain management strategies may include:

Minimally invasive surgery represents a significant advancement in surgical care, offering patients the benefits of reduced pain, shorter recovery times, and fewer complications. At Paragon Surgical Specialists in Concord, NC, the commitment to using the latest minimally invasive techniques ensures that patients receive the highest level of care with the least amount of discomfort. Whether you need a routine procedure like a hernia repair or a more complex surgery, the skilled team at Paragon Surgical Specialists is dedicated to helping you recover quickly and with minimal pain.

# Benefits of Minimally Invasive Surgery: Faster Recovery, Less Pain, More Confidence

## *How Robotic-Assisted Techniques Are Transforming Healing*

Surgery can feel like a big step, but thanks to advances in technology, it doesn’t have to mean a long hospital stay, a large scar, or weeks of downtime. **Minimally invasive surgery (MIS)**, especially when performed using robotic-assisted tools like the [**Da Vinci Surgical System**](/robotic-surgery), is changing how patients experience healing.

At **St. Mary’s**, we’re proud to offer this advanced approach to many types of procedures, combining **the precision of modern robotics with the compassion of personalized care**. Here’s how minimally invasive surgery can support your recovery and your confidence, every step of the way.

![What Is Minimally Invasive Surgery?](/sites/default/files/inline-images/robotic%20surgery_1.jpg)

![What Is Minimally Invasive Surgery?](/sites/default/files/inline-images/robotic%20surgery_1.jpg)

## What Is Minimally Invasive Surgery?

Minimally invasive surgery uses **small incisions**, advanced instruments, and often a camera or robotic system to perform complex procedures with **less disruption to your body**. Instead of the large incisions used in traditional open surgery, most MIS procedures are done through openings no larger than a dime.

## What Is Robotic-Assisted Surgery?

With robotic-assisted surgery, like the Da Vinci system, your surgeon operates **using a console and robotic arms** that offer precise control and high-definition, 3D visualization. Despite the name, **your surgeon is in full control at all times**.

**This technique allows for:**

## Let’s look at the real benefits that matter most to patients:

### Smaller Incisions, Less Scarring

Most procedures use 1–3 small incisions, leading to minimal scarring and faster healing of the skin and soft tissues.

### Less Pain and Fewer Complications

Because less muscle and tissue is disturbed, you’ll likely experience less pain, less blood loss, and a lower risk of infection.

### Shorter Hospital Stays

Many minimally invasive surgeries are performed on an outpatient basis or require just a short hospital stay compared to traditional procedures.

### Faster Recovery Times

Many patients return to work, daily routines, and physical activities much sooner, sometimes in weeks rather than months.

### More Confidence During Recovery

With less discomfort and a quicker return to normal, many patients feel more positive and in control during recovery.

## Common Procedures That May Be Minimally Invasive

At St. Mary’s, we use minimally invasive and robotic-assisted techniques for a variety of surgeries, including:

Your surgeon will discuss whether MIS is appropriate based on your unique condition and health history.

![Common Procedures That May Be Minimally Invasive](/sites/default/files/inline-images/Arthritis%202_3.jpg)

![What to Expect After Robotic-Assisted Surgery](/sites/default/files/inline-images/Beat%20the%20Heat%20Summer%20Health%20Tips%20from%20Your%20Local%20Primary%20Care%20Team_1.jpg)

![What to Expect After Robotic-Assisted Surgery](/sites/default/files/inline-images/Beat%20the%20Heat%20Summer%20Health%20Tips%20from%20Your%20Local%20Primary%20Care%20Team_1.jpg)

## What to Expect After Robotic-Assisted Surgery

While every procedure is different, patients who have robotic-assisted surgery often report:

It’s important to follow your surgeon’s recovery instructions and attend follow-up appointments to monitor healing.

## Talk to Your Provider About Surgical Options

Not every surgery can be done with a minimally invasive technique—but many can. The best way to know your options is to have a conversation with your provider. Together, you can discuss:

St. Mary's offers multiple locations where you can discuss your surgical options. Speak to a primary care provider today at one of our primary care offices and begin your healing journey today.

[View All St. Mary's Locations](/locations)

![Talk to Your Provider About Surgical Options](/sites/default/files/inline-images/St.%20Mary%27s%20Internal%20Medicine%20Associates.jpg)

![Talk to Your Provider About Surgical Options](/sites/default/files/inline-images/St.%20Mary%27s%20Internal%20Medicine%20Associates.jpg)

## Technology That Heals. People Who Care.

Minimally invasive and robotic-assisted surgeries offer more than just advanced techniques; they offer hope, comfort, and confidence to patients ready to take the next step. At St. Mary’s, you don’t have to choose between cutting-edge medicine and heartfelt care; you get both.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Minimally invasive surgery (MIS) results in significantly less postoperative pain due to smaller incisions, leading to reduced tissue trauma and lower reliance on pain medications.
Patients undergoing MIS typically experience faster recovery, often returning to daily activities within weeks rather than months, thanks to minimized tissue damage and quicker healing.
The precision of minimally invasive techniques results in fewer complications and lower infection risks, contributing to a safer surgical experience for patients.
With enhanced accuracy and less postoperative pain, MIS leads to better overall patient outcomes, including greater satisfaction and quicker return to normal life.
Minimally invasive surgeries often require shorter hospital stays, resulting in significant cost savings for healthcare systems, patients, and insurers alike.
